title: Inductive coupling
abstract: An inductive coupler includes a first transceiver electrically coupled to a first coil through a first tuning circuit. The inductive coupler may also include a second transceiver electrically coupled to a second coil through a second tuning circuit, where the second coil is positioned substantially concentric with the first coil.
claims: Claims An inductive coupler comprising: a first transceiver electrically coupled to a first coil through a first tuning circuit; and a second transceiver electrically coupled to a second coil through a second tuning circuit, the second coil positioned substantially concentric with the first coil. The inductive coupler of claim 1, wherein the first tuning circuit and second tuning circuit comprise one or more of parallel resonant tanks or series resonant tanks. The inductive coupler of claim 2, wherein the first tuning circuit has a resonant frequency substantially equal to a resonant frequency of the second tuning circuit. The inductive coupler of claim 3, wherein the first tuning circuit comprises a first capacitor having a first capacitance and wherein the second tuning circuit comprises a second capacitor having a second capacitance. The inductive coupler of claim 4, wherein the first capacitance and second capacitance are selected with respect to a first inductance of the first coil and a second inductance of the second coil such that the resonant frequency of the first tuning circuit is substantially equal to the resonant frequency of the second tuning circuit. The inductive coupler of claim 2, wherein the first and second tuning circuits are single- tuned or dual-tuned. 7. The inductive coupler of claim 6, wherein the first and second tuning circuits are dual-tuned, and signal and power is transferred through the inductive coupler. 8. The inductive coupler of claim 2, wherein the first and second tuning circuits comprise one or more low-pass filters, high-pass filters, or band rejection filters. 9. The inductive coupler of claim 2, wherein the first and second tuning circuits comprise one or more impedance-matching circuits. 10. The inductive coupler of claim 1, wherein the first transceiver is positioned in a collar and the second transceiver is positioned in a probe positioned within the collar. 11. A downhole tool comprising: a collar including a primary collar and an intermediate collar; a probe positioned within the collar; a collar transceiver positioned within the primary collar, the collar transceiver electrically coupled to a male coil through a collar tuning circuit; a probe transceiver positioned within the probe, the probe transceiver electrically coupled to a probe coil through a probe tuning circuit; a female coil positioned within the intermediate collar; and a collar coil positioned within the intermediate collar, the collar coil electrically coupled to the female coil through an intermediate tuning circuit. 12. The downhole tool of claim 11, wherein the female coil is substantially concentric with the mail coil. 13. The downhole tool of claim 11, further comprising a threaded connection between the primary collar and the intermediate collar, the threaded connection including a male coupler and a female coupler, wherein the male coil is positioned on the male coupler and the female coil is positioned on the female coupler. 14. The downhole tool of claim 11, wherein the collar tuning circuit and probe tuning circuits comprise one or more of parallel resonant tanks or series resonant tanks, and the intermediate tuning circuit comprises one or more of parallel resonant tanks or series resonant tanks. 15. The downhole tool of claim 11, wherein the intermediate tuning circuit comprises a primary tuning circuit and a secondary tuning circuit. 16. The downhole tool of claim 15, wherein the collar tuning circuit has a resonant frequency substantially equal to a resonant frequency of the primary tuning circuit, and the probe tuning circuit has a resonant frequency substantially equal to a resonant frequency of the secondary tuning circuit. 17. The downhole tool of claim 16, wherein the first, second, primary, and secondary tuning circuits each comprise a capacitor. 18. The downhole tool of claim 16, wherein the first and second tuning circuits are single-tuned or dual -tuned. 19. The downhole tool of claim 18, wherein the first and second tuning circuits are dual -tuned, and signal and power is transferred through the inductive coupler. 20. The downhole tool of claim 16, wherein the first and second tuning circuits comprise one or more low-pass filters, high-pass filters, or band rejection filters. 21. The downhole tool of claim 16, wherein the first and second tuning circuits comprise one or more impedance-matching circuits. 22. The downhole tool of claim 11, wherein the collar and probe are components of a motor, turbine, rotary steerable system, MWD, LWD, or drill bit and the probe is a mandrel. 23. A centralizer for supporting a probe within a collar of a downhole tool comprising: a centralizer body; an outer coil, the outer coil positioned at an outer surface of the centralizer body; and an inner coil, the inner coil positioned at an inner surface of the centralizer body, the inner coil electrically coupled to the outer coil through a centralizer tuning circuit. 24. The centralizer of claim 23, wherein the centralizer tuning circuit comprises one or more of parallel resonant tanks or series resonant tanks. 25. The centralizer of claim 23, wherein the centralizer tuning circuit comprises a primary tuning circuit and a secondary tuning circuit. 26. The centralizer of claim 25, wherein primary, and secondary tuning circuits each comprises a capacitor. 27. The centralizer of claim 25, wherein the primary tuning circuit has a resonant frequency substantially equal to a resonant frequency of a first tuning circuit positioned in the collar, and the secondary tuning circuit has a resonant frequency substantially equal to a resonant frequency of a tuning circuit in the probe. 28. The centralizer of claim 23, wherein the centralizer body is formed from a nonmagnetic material. 29. The centralizer of claim 20, wherein the centralizer body is formed from rubber. 30. The centralizer of claim 23, wherein the centralizer body comprises an inner annular segment and an outer annular segment, the inner and outer annular segments mechanically coupled by one or more spokes. 31. The centralizer of claim 30, wherein the inner coil is positioned within the inner annular segment and the outer coil is positioned within the outer annular segment. 32. The centralizer of claim 30, comprising a flowpath formed between the inner annular segment and the outer annular segment.
